items.title: Rider killed in Finke Desert Race crash identified as Matthew Bielefeld

children.content: A motorcyclist who died while competing in this weekend's Finke Desert Race outside Alice Springs has been identified as Matthew Bielefeld.

articles.title: Famous outback race claims another life after serious motorbike crash

children.content: A motorbike rider has died in the Central Australian outback after a Finke Desert Race crash, with the two-day event expected to continue as police investigate.

image.altText: A row of motorbikes at the starting line, their tyres are kicking up clumps of the dirt track.

gallery.altText: Two photos of a brunette man smiling, he is alongside a motorbike in one image and standing with a blonde woman in the other.

thumbnail.altText: Two photos of a brunette man smiling, he is alongside a motorbike in one image and standing with a blonde woman in the other.

items.title: Spear-throwing, music, dance and some notable absences at Barunga Festival 2026

children.content: Sammy Wurrawilya has taken out the title of spear-throwing champion at this year's Barunga Festival with an impressive effort of more than 30 metres.

image.altText: A traditional Aboriginal dancer.

items.title: Kings and Queens of the Desert crowned as outback Finke race ends

children.content: Returning legends of the Finke Desert Race, David Walsh and Travis Robinson, have been crowned Kings of the Desert at the gruelling two-day off-road race, with Walsh winning on bikes for a record-equalling sixth time and Robinson claiming victory on cars.

image.altText: A small group of people in car racing outfits standing together on a stage and smiling, with one holding a winner's shield high.

items.title: Marilyn's world 'fell apart' with one post. What happened next helped her heal

children.content: Marilyn was just five when she first encountered grief. Now 18, the teenager says she's been to more funerals than birthday parties.

image.altText: A young woman with a flower tucked behind her ear smiles as she holds onto her necklace.

items.title: Indigenous families want 'crisis' investigated after 15 deaths in six months

children.content: Remote Indigenous communities across Australia are painfully aware that their life expectancy rates are worsening — and in one Northern Territory community, residents are calling for an investigation into a local death rate they feel is "not normal".

image.altText: Aboriginal senior woman and younger Aboriginal man sitting together, serious expression, in front of green leaves.

items.title: Young couple told by agent that landlords would not rent to 'people like you'

children.content: Hopeful renters say negative stereotypes about young people are making it almost impossible to secure a rental on the Sunshine Coast.

items.title: $3,000 murder contracts and 'violence brokers' fuelling underworld bloodshed

children.content: One of the state's top police officers has promised feuding gangs bringing terror to Sydney's streets will not exist "by the time we finish" as police stare down a new era of disorganised crime.

items.title: Evans's coming out took extraordinary courage and the NRL must match it

children.content: Kane Evans showed remarkable bravery to reveal his sexuality to the world and now rugby league must lift to his level.

items.title: Four-time veterans to late bolters, here's your Socceroos pocket guide

children.content: Mat Ryan and Mathew Leckie are at a record-equalling fourth FIFA World Cup, while Cristian Volpato headlines a host of first-timers, after his late switch from Italy. Here's your complete guide to the Socceroos' World Cup squad.

image.altText: Three soccer players embracae in green and gold.

items.title: Family's 'worst nightmare' after purchasing home filled with defects

children.content: A young family invested their life savings into their dream home only to discover it was filled with defects, as the state's consumer watchdog issues a public warning notice about the builder responsible and seven other traders.

items.title: 'Everything's on hold': Patients caught in surgery backlog

children.content: Patients are waiting years for elective surgery as delays and cancellations mount in Tasmania — can the health system handle the $700 million of cuts still to come?

children.content: Plague locusts have moved into north-west Victoria, with numbers reaching their highest levels in 15 years.

items.title: As One Nation nips at his heels the PM has no choice but to play the long game

children.content: The government is playing a long game in responding to the surge of support for One Nation. It has little other choice.

items.title: At least 35 dead after magnitude-7.8 earthquake hits Philippines

children.content: At least 35 people have been killed in a magnitude-7.8 earthquake that shook the Mindanao region in the southern Philippines, with a tsunami possible on some regional coasts.

items.title: Script reveals methods of Cambodian centre scamming Australians

items.title: Xi and Kim pledge deeper cooperation between China and North Korea

Chinese leader Xi Jinping has suggested during a rare two-day visit to Pyongyang.

items.title: Pope Leo meets abuse victims in Spain while some protest

children.content: The pontiff, who is on a week-long visit to Spain, has listened to proposals to make safeguarding more effective.

items.title: A 'ring of fire' could soon burn around one of Australia's major cities

children.content: Australian states are pushing ahead with waste-to-energy incineration despite significant opposition. It means one capital city will soon be surrounded by a "ring of fire".

items.title: Hitchhiking wallaby returns home after 2,000km round trip

children.content: Roadkill is a common sight on outback roads, but a group of travellers was dedicated to making sure a joey found in north Queensland survived.
